primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivable for foreign currency hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs set the eligibility criteria for hedged items in hedge accounting, including the exception allowing intragroup monetary items to be hedged for foreign currency risk in consolidated statements.
These paragraphs establish the documentation and effectiveness requirements for applying hedge accounting.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain the treatment of foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items and when they are recognized in profit or loss or other comprehensive income.
These paragraphs address the recognition of gains and losses on financial instruments, including foreign currency risk.

peripheral_authority.reason: This interpretation provides guidance on net investment hedges, which is a different type of hedge and not directly applicable to intragroup dividend receivables.
This standard deals with disclosures about interests in other entities, but does not govern the eligibility of hedge accounting for intragroup items.

treatment_families.family: Foreign currency risk hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items (exception to external party rule)
Foreign currency risk hedge accounting for forecast intragroup transactions

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk on intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This approach is permitted under the exception in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 for intragroup monetary items where foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk on forecast intragroup dividend
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This approach is available for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions denominated in a foreign currency, as per IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.3, though not applicable to already recognized receivables.
